content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

March 6, 1990


Anthony L. Miller
Office of the Secretary of State
1230 J Street
Sacramento, CA  95814

Re:  Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No. I-90-138

Dear Mr. Miller:

You have requested informal assistance with respect to the
mass mailing provisions of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").¹

QUESTION

May the name and signature of the Secretary of State be in
the certificate of correctness which is required to be included in
the election ballot pamphlet pursuant to Section 88005(d)?

CONCLUSION

The name and signature of the Secretary of State may be
included in the election ballot pamphlet in the certificate of
correctness, because the certificate is required by law.

FACTS

Prior to the passage of Proposition 73, you were advised that
the name and signature of the Secretary of State could appear in
the certificate of correctness required by Section 88005(d)

__________________________
1  Government Code Sections 81000-91015.  All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.  Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations Section
18000, et seq.  All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice.
(Government Code Section 83114; 2 Cal. Code of Regs.
Section 18329(c)(3).)


428 J Street, Suite 800 ● P.O. Box 807 ● Sacramento CA 95804-0807 ● (916) 322-5660

Our File No. I-90-138
Page 2

(Miller Advice Letter No. A-88-257, copy enclosed). You have
submitted portions of the 1990 sample ballot pamphlet and wish to
know if the use of the Secretary of State's name and signature is
still appropriate.

ANALYSIS

Section 89001 of the Act provides that "no newsletter or
other mass mailing shall be sent at public expense." A mass mailing is defined as two hundred or more substantially similar pieces
of mail. (Section 82041.5.) Regulation 18901 clarifies that the
purpose of Section 89001 is to prevent elected officers from using
public funds to send out newsletters and other mass mailings at
public expense. A mass mailing may not be sent at public expense
if it contains the name, office, photograph or any other reference
to an elected officer affiliated with the agency producing or
sending the mailing and is prepared or sent in cooperation,
consultation, coordination or concert with the elected officer.
(Regulation 18901(a).)

The regulation contains an exception for legal notices or
other items sent as required by law in which the use of the
elected officer's name, office, title, or signature is necessary
in the notice or other mailing. (Regulation 18901(b)(7).) The
certificate of correctness required by Section 88005(d) comes
within this exception. We have reviewed the proposed format of
your certificate and believe that, with one exception, the use of
the Secretary of State's name and signature has been limited to
what is reasonably required to be included in such a certificate.
The one exception is that the Secretary of State's title should
not be included in the name of the certificate. This will confirm
that, pursuant to our telephone conversation of February 16, 1990,
you have now changed the certificate to read "Certificate of
Correctness."

The portions of the sample ballot provided contained the
title "Secretary of State" in several other places. In our
telephone conversation of February 16, those uses were discussed
as follows:

1. The title could be used in the letterhead of the general
letter describing the contents of the pamphlet and emphasizing the
importance of voting. The regulation contains an exception

2 A revised version of Regulation 18901 was adopted by the Commission on December 13, 1989, and a statutorily required public
comment period expired January 17, 1990. Although the revised
version of the regulation is still in the rulemaking process, it
represents the policy of the Commission. Therefore, we are advising agencies that they may rely on it. The advice contained in
this letter is based upon the revised version of Regulation 18901,
a copy of which is enclosed.

Our File No. I-90-138
Page 3

permitting use of the elected officer's name in the letterhead of
the agency stationery. (Section 18901(b)(1).)

2. The title cannot be used at the end of the letter in
place of a signature. (Section 18901(b)(1).)

3. The title must be removed from the outside of the
pamphlet, except for the following:

a. It may appear in the return address of the form,
since the form is a mailer used in place of an agency
envelope. (Section 18901(b)(1).)

b. It may appear in the postage meter stamp, based upon
your representation that this use is required by postal law.
(Section 18901(b)(7).)

This will confirm that the modifications provided to us in
accordance with the above advice comply with the requirements of
Section 89001 and Regulation 18901.

If you have any questions regarding the above, you may
contact me at (916) 322-5901.

Sincerely,

Kathryn E. Donovan
General Counsel

[signature]

By: Margaret W. Ellison
Counsel, Legal Division

KED:MWE:aa

Enclosure
